args_redacted_json.instruction: Prepare approximately 15 Beamer slides covering the referenced paper for an ML research audience.

result_summary_json.contribution: Introduces OptimusVLA, a dual-memory Vision-Language-Action framework that accelerates and stabilizes robotic manipulation by replacing isotropic noise with retrieved task-level priors (Global Prior Memory) and enforcing temporal coherence via historical action modeling (Local Consistency Memory).
result_summary_json.method: The system encodes multimodal inputs to retrieve semantically similar task trajectories using Global Prior Memory (GPM), adjusting noise scales and denoising steps dynamically based on semantic similarity. Concurrently, Local Consistency Memory (LCM) encodes the historical action sequence to compute a temporal consistency offset. This offset and the generated prior are combined to initialize the action generation process, bridging the distributional gap and dramatically reducing the required number of function evaluations (NFEs) for the generative policy.
key_results: Achieves a 98.6% average success rate on the LIBERO simulation benchmark.
Outperforms \pi_0 by 13.5% on CALVIN and achieves a 38% average success rate on RoboTwin 2.0 Hard.
Surpasses \pi_0 by 42.9% on real-world Generalization Tasks and 52.4% on Long-horizon Tasks.
Delivers a 2.9x inference speedup over standard models by significantly reducing generative denoising steps through adaptive priors.

result_summary_json.title: OptimusVLA: A Dual-Memory Vision-Language-Action Framework for Efficient Robotic Manipulation

slides.title: Fusing Dual Memories for Action Generation

result_summary_json.frame: \begin{frame} \frametitle{Executive Summary} \begin{itemize} \item Novel dual-memory architecture replaces inefficient iterative denoising. \item Achieves 98.6\% average success rate on LIBERO benchmark. \item Outperforms standard models by 52.4\% on real-world long-horizon tasks. \item Delivers 2.9x inference speedup with fewer denoising steps. \end{itemize} \end{frame}

\begin{frame}{OptimusVLA: Framework Overview} \begin{columns} \begin{column}{0.5\textwidth} \begin{itemize} \item V-L backbone generates multimodal input representations. \item Global Prior Memory retrieves semantic task priors. \item Local Consistency Memory models historical action sequences. \item GPM and LCM initialize generation, bypassing pure noise. \end{itemize} \end{column} \begin{column}{0.5\textwidth} \centering \includegraphics[width=0.85\textwidth,height=0.7\textheight,keepaspectratio]{p0-fig-002} \end{column} \end{columns} \end{frame}

\begin{frame}{透過語義相似度動態適應} \begin{itemize} \item 動態調整噪聲比例與去噪步數。 \item 基於檢索先驗的語義相似度分數決定。 \item 高置信度先驗能顯著降低去噪步數與噪聲。 \item 兼顧已知任務效率與未知任務彈性。 \end{itemize} \begin{equation*} \lambda=\lambda_{\max}-\tfrac{\bar s+1}{2}\,(\lambda_{\max}-\lambda_{\min}), N=N_{\min}+\big(1-\tfrac{\bar s+1}{2} \big)\,(N_{\max}-N_{\min}) \end{equation*} \end{frame}

\begin{frame} \frametitle{Initializing the Generative Prior} \begin{itemize} \item Retrieved mean and scaled variance construct target prior state. \item Replaces standard normal distributions used in typical diffusion models. \end{itemize} \begin{equation*} \mathbf{\hat{X}}_{t}=\mu+\lambda\,\big(\epsilon\odot\sqrt{\mathrm{Var}}\big), \qquad \epsilon\sim\mathcal{N}(0,I) \end{equation*} \begin{itemize} \item Provides strong anchor states requiring only minimal refinement. \item Improves generation quality and massively reduces computational cost. \end{itemize} \end{frame}

\begin{frame} \frametitle{Local Consistency Memory: Temporal Coherence} \begin{itemize} \item Encodes recent sequence of executed actions. \item Calculates temporal consistency offset for smooth transitions. \item Mitigates jittery movements common in standard VLAs. \item Bridges independent predictions and continuous physical control. \end{itemize} \end{frame}

args_redacted_json.slide_title: Fusing Dual Memories for Action Generation

result_summary_json.frame: \begin{frame} \frametitle{Experimental Setup \& Benchmarks} \begin{itemize} \item Evaluated on LIBERO, CALVIN, and RoboTwin 2.0 Hard simulations. \item Real-world tests cover Generalization and Long-horizon task suites. \item Compared against SOTA baselines: OpenVLA, OpenVLA-OFT, and $\pi_0$. \item Primary evaluation metrics: task success rate and inference efficiency. \end{itemize} \end{frame}

\begin{frame} \frametitle{Results: Superior Simulation Performance} \begin{itemize} \item 98.6\% average success rate on LIBERO benchmark. \item Outperforms $\pi_0$ baseline by 13.5\% on CALVIN. \item 38\% average success rate on RoboTwin 2.0 Hard. \item Robust dual-memory priors across diverse simulation physics. \end{itemize} \end{frame}

\begin{frame}{Results: Real-World Generalization} \begin{itemize} \item Surpasses $\pi_0$ by 42.9\% on Generalization Tasks. \item Beats $\pi_0$ by 52.4\% on Long-horizon Tasks. \item Robust zero-shot transfer to unseen physical environments. \item LCM effectively prevents error accumulation in long sequences. \end{itemize} \begin{center} \includegraphics[width=0.85\textwidth,height=0.7\textheight,keepaspectratio]{p0-fig-003} \end{center} \end{frame}

\begin{frame}{Conclusion} \begin{itemize} \item Introduces dual-memory architecture for robotic manipulation. \item GPM provides accurate global priors, bypassing isotropic noise. \item LCM guarantees local temporal consistency for smooth execution. \item Achieves 2.9x faster inference and new SOTA benchmarks. \end{itemize} \end{frame}

result_summary_json.title: Global Prior Meets Local Consistency: Dual-Memory Augmented Vision-Language-Action Model for Efficient Robotic Manipulation
result_summary_json.author: Li, Hu, Shao, et al.
